title: The Bookend Rei
wikitext:
[]: The "Bookend" Rei             Quote Post by Chroma » Sun Oct 02, 2011 3:33 pm This was probably already discussed once, but here I go anyway: I was watching 1.11 again last night and I noticed that, in the beginning, the first "bookend" Rei was still there. Now, if the Rebuild series was closer to the original series, I wouldn't think much of this. But it isn't. It's very different (and it seems like it will continue this way in 3.0 and Final). So why do you think the "bookend" Rei is still there? Do you think that she'll/they'll be there at the end of the Rebuild series too?

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Oct 02, 2011 4:04 pm like most of the original NGE references in rebuild the "Bookend" Rei is most likely a red herring, similar to the blood stain on the moon or the tree of life in the water treatment plant. they also reference EoE with the injuries Asuka receives from the dummy plug incident, they mirror the injuries in one last final's beach scene .

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Oct 02, 2011 4:07 pm It may suggest that Rei will be dead by the end again. Alternatively, it could just be a statement of the obvious, that this is where someone (the viewer) starts to watch Shinji. Either way, I would obviously expect the other bookend to show up at some point.

[]:             Quote Post by Fallingtogrey » Sun Oct 02, 2011 5:00 pm Hopefully he will.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Oct 02, 2011 5:34 pm Fallingtogrey: 1.11 aleady shows differences from NGE by having Shinji experiences Hell Train visions after his first battle--among other things, so I don't think that's the case. (1.xx and 2.xx were worked on one after another/back to back as well, according to the 2.0 Complete Records Collection.) What's interesting is Bookend Rei actually blinks (updated animation of NGE Rei's OP blink) when appearing before Shinji, and there's a haze in front of her (AT Field or something else??) as well. Sequence of events: Bookend Rei  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Chroma » Sun Oct 02, 2011 5:56 pm I took that "haze" to be something heat-related, but you bring up a good point. It could very well be an AT-field or something. Was that haze there in NGE as well, or only rebuild?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Oct 02, 2011 5:58 pm http://wiki.evageeks.org/FGC:Episode_01_Scene_01 Something of a haze, it seems like.

[]:             Quote Post by Chroma » Sun Oct 02, 2011 6:25 pm There is a bit of a haze, though not as much as in Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Mon Oct 03, 2011 4:18 am It's probably just to give her a mirage-like appearance.

[]:             Quote Post by User-iel » Mon Oct 03, 2011 4:33 am @ Sailor Star Dust Hmm. Since you informed me about the production schedule, it seems right to proceed as if there is a firmly held plan or outline, not an environment of improvisation. Then I will present the question in more fundamental terms, if you would like. NME is different. If it is different to the extent that it's no longer "world type," depending on the nature of the plot as AU or not, we might not have seen Bookend Rei at all. Rei might still die/become quantum, but if the world is not reset, she's functionally more like Bookmark Rei. @ chroma - And I'm going to suppose yes, she acts out the role of observer once more. Because "Eva is a story that repeats" sounds nice.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Oct 03, 2011 2:38 pm Since EOE Rei transcends time and space, she can just casually show up in other universes whenever she wants. Kaworu can do it, why not? Plot hole preemptively filled, motherfucker.

[]:             Quote Post by Chroma » Mon Oct 03, 2011 9:21 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Since EOE Rei transcends time and space, she can just casually show up in other universes whenever she wants. Kaworu can do it, why not? Plot hole preemptively filled, motherfucker.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Oct 03, 2011 9:46 pm Chroma wrote: When was that whole "transcends time and space" thing mentioned?

[]:             Quote Post by Fallingtogrey » Tue Oct 04, 2011 5:29 am Possibly?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Oct 04, 2011 9:20 am Fallingtogrey wrote: I only say this because according to an interview in another thread, Anno had to be told to change things in 2.0. Like Asuka taking Toji's place in a certain "plot point". He had to be told to deviate from NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Oct 04, 2011 12:24 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Anno's original script didn't have Shinji meet up with Kensuke camping outside.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Oct 04, 2011 7:30 pm Wow, Anno let them write a whole episode? That's... kinda cool.
